section_name: Seizure of Stolen Vehicle or Vehicle with Altered Vehicle Identification Number
text:
(a) A peace officer may seize a vehicle or part of a vehicle without a warrant if the officer has probable cause to believe that the vehicle or part:

(1) is stolen; or

(2) has had the serial number removed, altered, or obliterated.

(b) A vehicle or part seized under this section may be treated as stolen property for purposes of custody and disposition of the vehicle or part.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 501. Certificate of Title Act
##### § 501.158 — Seizure of Stolen Vehicle or Vehicle with Altered Vehicle Identification Number

(a) A peace officer may seize a vehicle or part of a vehicle without a warrant if the officer has probable cause to believe that the vehicle or part:

    (1) is stolen; or

    (2) has had the serial number removed, altered, or obliterated.

(b) A vehicle or part seized under this section may be treated as stolen property for purposes of custody and disposition of the vehicle or part.

*Acts 2011, 82nd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1296 (H.B.2357), Sec. 67, eff. January 1, 2012.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A peace officer may seize a vehicle or part of a vehicle without a warrant if the officer has probable cause to believe that the vehicle or part:
has had the serial number removed, altered, or obliterated.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A vehicle or part seized under this section may be treated as stolen property for purposes of custody and disposition of the vehicle or part.
